context: Tests whether the agent writes an Artillery load test configuration that follows the skill's guidance on phase structure, scenario weights, response capture for request chaining, and processor-based test data generation.

checklist.name: Warm-up phase
checklist.description: Artillery config includes a warm-up phase (low arrivalRate, e.g., 5 rps, ~60s duration)

checklist.name: Ramp-up phase
checklist.description: Artillery config includes a ramp-up phase with a `rampTo` field going from low to ~50 rps

checklist.name: Peak load phase
checklist.description: Artillery config includes a sustained peak load phase (~50 rps for ~300s duration)

checklist.name: Spike phase
checklist.description: Artillery config includes a brief spike phase with significantly higher arrivalRate (e.g., 200 rps for ~30s)

checklist.name: Recovery phase
checklist.description: Artillery config includes a recovery phase after the spike with reduced arrivalRate

checklist.name: Browse scenario weight 60
checklist.description: The catalog browsing scenario has a weight of 60

checklist.name: Checkout scenario weight
checklist.description: The checkout scenario has a weight of 20 (or close to 20)

checklist.name: Response capture
checklist.description: At least one scenario uses `capture` with a `json` path expression to extract a value (e.g., productId, cartId) from a response and passes it to a later step

checklist.name: Processor file exists
checklist.description: A processor JavaScript file exists at artillery/processors/commerce-helpers.js (or referenced path)

checklist.name: Processor generates email
checklist.description: The processor function sets context.vars.email to a unique address using a non-production domain (e.g., @test.invalid)

checklist.name: Processor generates productId
checklist.description: The processor function sets context.vars.productId (or similar) to a dynamically generated product identifier

checklist.name: Think time in checkout
checklist.description: The checkout flow includes at least one `think` step between requests to simulate form-filling or user delay
